name: Refugio County
description_short: Refugio County Federal Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit financial institution serving three school districts in Refugio County since 1958.
description_long:
Refugio County Federal Credit Union was established in 1958 as a federally insured, member-owned financial institution dedicated to promoting the financial well-being of its members. As a federal credit union, it operates under NCUA insurance and maintains a not-for-profit structure, distinguishing it from traditional for-profit banks. The institution has maintained continuous service to its community for over six decades, reflecting stability and long-term commitment to member relationships.

The credit union serves a defined membership base of three school districts within Refugio County: Refugio ISD, Woodsboro ISD, and Austwell-Tivoli ISD. This focused membership model allows the institution to tailor services and understand the specific financial needs of educators and school-district-affiliated individuals. The credit union offers member.net home banking services, providing digital access to accounts and financial management tools for remote and convenient banking.

Refugio County FCU distinguishes itself through its community-focused mission and eligibility requirements tied to school district employment. Unlike national banks, this credit union prioritizes member relationships and reinvests profits back into member benefits rather than shareholder dividends. The institution's longevity and exclusive service to a defined community demonstrates commitment to member loyalty and personalized financial service.

The main limitation of Refugio County FCU is its restricted membership eligibility—only individuals affiliated with the three specified school districts qualify to join. This geographic and employment-based restriction significantly narrows the potential customer base compared to community banks or national credit unions. Additionally, the website provides limited details about specific loan products, interest rates, fees, or comprehensive service offerings, making it difficult to assess competitive advantages in rates or terms.
services: Share accounts (deposit accounts for credit union members)
Member.Net home banking platform for online account access
Account login and personal financial management
Federal NCUA insurance on member deposits
pros: Federally insured (NCUA) member-owned not-for-profit structure ensures safety and member-focused operations
Over 65 years of continuous service and stability in Refugio County community
Member.Net home banking platform provides digital account access and online banking convenience
Focused membership model tied to school districts ensures understanding of educators' specific financial needs
No-profit reinvestment policy means earnings benefit members rather than external shareholders
Local presence with direct phone contact (361.526.2275) for personalized service
Clear membership eligibility based on school district affiliation removes uncertainty for qualified members
cons: Highly restrictive membership eligibility limited to three specific school districts in Refugio County only
Website lacks detailed information about loan products, interest rates, fees, or APRs for comparison
Limited service area and geographic footprint restricts convenience for members outside Refugio County
No mention of specific share account types, certificate rates, or competitive product offerings
Website provides minimal details about alternative services such as credit cards, mortgages, or business loans
best_for: Educators and employees of Refugio ISD, Woodsboro ISD, or Austwell-Tivoli ISD seeking member-owned banking
Refugio County residents with school district employment eligibility looking for community-focused financial services
Members prioritizing not-for-profit financial institutions and local relationship banking
diagnosis: Refugio County FCU is best for educators and school district employees in three specific Texas school districts seeking a stable, member-owned credit union with local service. The primary caveat is strict membership eligibility requirements that exclude most consumers outside the three designated school districts.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
